summary: Frontend testing: 100% pass. The localStorage fallback fix in /app/frontend/src/api.ts successfully resolved the expo-secure-store web compatibility issue. All requested flows tested and working: login → home tabs navigation, home feed with sections, spot detail page, tab navigation (Home/Explore/Add/Saved/Profile), paywall screen, profile screen, and saved sub-tabs.

react_native_issues.issue: React Native deprecation warning: 'Image: style.resizeMode is deprecated. Please use props.resizeMode.' This appears in multiple components but does not affect functionality.

action_items: Optional: Update Image components to use props.resizeMode instead of style.resizeMode to remove deprecation warnings
critical_code_review_comments: ✓ localStorage fallback in /app/frontend/src/api.ts working perfectly for web platform
✓ Login flow successfully stores JWT token in localStorage on web (Platform.OS === 'web')
✓ Home feed displays all sections (Nearby, Trending, Golden Hour) with Texas spot cards
✓ Spot detail page renders hero carousel, score rings, and reviews correctly
✓ Paywall screen displays all three tiers (Free, Pro, Elite) with proper styling
✓ Profile screen shows user info, Creator Dashboard, and Upgrade to Pro buttons
✓ Saved tab has all three sub-tabs (Favorites, Collections, Private)
✓ Explore tab shows spot grid with search and filters
✓ Add tab opens multi-step spot creation form
✓ All testID attributes present for critical interactive elements
✓ No SecureStore errors on web preview
seed_data_creation: Using existing seeded data from iteration 1: 1 admin (admin@photoscout.app), 5 photographers, 21 Texas spots
context_for_next_testing_agent: Frontend is fully functional. The localStorage fallback fix resolved the SecureStore web compatibility issue. All core flows tested and passing: login, home feed, spot detail, tab navigation, paywall, profile, saved tabs. Minor deprecation warning for Image.resizeMode can be addressed in future iteration if desired.
rca_of_issue: Issue from iteration 1 RESOLVED: The expo-secure-store web compatibility issue was fixed by adding Platform.OS === 'web' checks in /app/frontend/src/api.ts (lines 9-28). The fix uses window.localStorage for web and SecureStore for native platforms. Login now works correctly on web preview, storing JWT token in localStorage and successfully navigating to home tabs.
